data.claim: ISIC 2020 melanoma classifier achieves ROC-AUC > 0.70 on the challenge test set.

data.name: Duplicate-image leakage
data.reason: ISIC 2020 is publicly known to contain hundreds of exact duplicates.

data.name: Patient-level leakage
data.reason: Same patient appearing in multiple images is common in dermoscopy datasets.

data.name: Data augmentation applied pre-split
data.reason: Some repros augment the full dataset then split.

input.command: wc -l /tmp/isic-demo/data/isic_metadata.csv && python -c "import pandas as pd; df=pd.read_csv('/tmp/isic-demo/data/isic_metadata.csv'); print('rows', len(df), 'unique_hashes', df.image_hash.nunique())"

data.description: Count duplicate hashes and check dedup before split.

data.name: Read
data.output:
...
def prepare(df, test_size=0.2):
    df = df.copy()
    df = pd.get_dummies(df, columns=CATEGORICAL, drop_first=True)
    y = df[TARGET]
    X = df.drop(columns=[TARGET, *ID_COLS])
    X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(X, y, test_size=test_size, random_state=RANDOM_STATE, stratify=y)
    return X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test

data.result: 4025 rows, 3500 unique hashes (525 dups). prepare_data.py calls train_test_split without any drop_duplicates. Confirmed leakage.

data.summary: Exact-duplicate-image leakage. Fix: drop_duplicates on image_hash before split.

input.old_string:
df = df.copy()
    df = pd.get_dummies(df, columns=CATEGORICAL, drop_first=True)
input.new_string:
df = df.drop_duplicates(subset=['image_hash'], keep='first').reset_index(drop=True).copy()
    df = pd.get_dummies(df, columns=CATEGORICAL, drop_first=True)

data.diff_summary: One-line addition: drop_duplicates on image_hash before split.

data.name: Bash
data.output:
METRIC_JSON: {"metric": "AUC", "value": 0.6522, "context": "ISIC 2020 melanoma classification (RF on metadata+image features)"}
ROC-AUC: 0.6522

data.context: Random Forest on held-out test set

data.markdown: The repo reports ROC-AUC 0.7153 on a held-out 20% of the ISIC 2020 metadata + image-features training set.
Source CSV has 4025 rows with 3500 unique image_hash values (525 exact duplicates). prepare_data.py splits without dedup, so duplicates span train/test folds.
One-line addition to prepare_data.py: `df = df.drop_duplicates(subset=['image_hash']).reset_index(drop=True)` before the one-hot encoding.
Exact-hash dedup does not address perceptual near-duplicates or patient-level leakage. Both recommended as follow-up PRs.
